Fig. 3: Qualitative Results across Different Mosaic Levels.
Fig. 12: Qualitative Result of Factorized Diffusion.
Fig. 7: Visualization of Guidance Ablation Results.
Fig. 5: Qualitative Results of Generative Photomosaic.

Fig. 2: Method Overview. We generate photomosaic images using a diffusion-based framework. During the noise initialization stage, each partial reference block (e.g. 96× 96) is expanded to proper resolution (e.g. 768× 768) using an integral-noise subsampling. At every denoising step, we align the color distribution of the evolving tile image with its corresponding reference block and apply a low-frequency guidance term to preserve coarse structural layout. As a result, the model produces tile images that both follow the structure of the reference image and comply with the target prompt.
